serStmt.serInfo: Statistics South Africa. Labour Force Survey Pilot: February 2000. [dataset]. Version 1.1. Pretoria: Statistics South Africa [producer], 2001. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2011. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/ft1v-w181

verStmt.version: v1.1: Edited, anonymised dataset for licensed distribution
verStmt.notes:
The South African February 2000 LFS dataset was originally released in 2001 as 3 data files (household, worker and general). A second version was downloaded from the Statistics South Africa website on 11 August 2011 by DataFirst. This version differed slightly from the originally obtained release in the following ways:
1. The suffix ” Feb2000” no longer appears on all variable names 2. Year and Month variables were added 3. Variable labels were altered. Previously, all variable labels were literal questions. Now the variable labels describe the variables.

stdyInfo.abstract:
The LFS is a twice-yearly rotating panel household survey, specifically designed to measure the dynamics of employment and unemployment in South Africa. It measures a variety of issues related to the labour market,including unemployment rates (official and expanded), according to standard definitions of the International Labour Organisation (ILO).
All editions of the LFS have been updated (some more than once) since their release. These version changes are detailed in a document available from DataFirst (in the "external documents" section titled "LFS 2000-2008 Collated Version Notes on the South African LFS").

sumDscr.universe: The LFS sample covers the non-institutional population except for workers' hostels. However, persons living in private dwelling units within institutions are also enumerated. For example, within a school compound, one would enumerate the schoolmaster's house and teachers' accommodation because these are private dwellings. Students living in a dormitory on the school compound would, however, be excluded.

stdyInfo.notes: Household characteristics, household listing, demographics, education, economic activity, work for pay, business ownership, unemployment, employers, main work activity in the past week, wages, salary, employment, migration

dataColl.sampProc:
The LFS is a twice-yearly rotating panel household survey. A rotating panel sample involves visiting the same dwelling units on a number of occasions (in this instance, five at most), and replacing a proportion of these dwelling units each round (in this instance 20%). New dwelling units are added to the sample to replace those that are taken out. This February 2000 dataset represents the pilot study for the LFS. A sample of 10 000 households was drawn in 1 574 enumerator areas (EAs) (that is 10 households in each of the 426 non-urban EAs and 5 households in each of the 1 148 urban EAs). A two-stage sampling procedure was applied and the sample was stratified, clustered and selected to meet the requirements of probability sampling.
The sample was based on the 1996 Population Census enumerator areas and the estimated number of households from the 1996 Population Census. The sampled population excluded all prisoners in prisons, patients in hospitals, people residing in boarding houses and hotels (whether temporary or semi-permanent). The sample was explicitly stratified by province and area type (urban/rural). Within each explicit stratum the EAs were further stratified by simply arranging them in geographical order by District Council, Magisterial District and, within the magisterial district, by average household income (for formal urban areas and hostels). The allocated number of EAs was systematically selected with probability proportional to size in each stratum. The measure of size was the estimated number of households in each EA. A systematic sample of 10 households in non-urban and 5 households in urban areas was then drawn.
dataColl.resInstru: Data collected includes data on households and person data (via the Flap and Section 1 of the questionnaire), worker data on persons 15-65 years (Sections 2, 3, 4 and 5). worker data collected includes labour market data, including employment in both the formal and informal sectors, and data on unemployment. Most questions in the Labour Force Survey questionnaire are pre-coded, i.e. there is a set number of choices from which one or more must be selected. Post-coding was done for open-ended questions.
dataColl.weight:
The 1996 population Census was used as a basis for the weighting. Household weights were calculated by using the reciprocal of the inclusion probabilities. To calculate the person weight, the data was post-stratified by province, gender and age group (5-year age groups). The 1996 Census figures (adjusted for growth) were used as benchmarks. Relative scaling was also done on this weight to cater for the population group.
Stats SA revised their population model to produce mid-year population estimates in light of mortality data released in 2005 (see Stats SA Statistical Release P0309.3, 2005). The benchmarks for the LFS discussed in this statistical release have been adjusted accordingly. Weights were then adjusted according to those 2005 population estimates for all LFS datasets EXCEPT THIS ONE (February 2000).

useStmt.citReq: Statistics South Africa. Labour Force Survey Pilot: February 2000. [dataset]. Version 1.1. Pretoria: Statistics South Africa [producer], 2001. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2011. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/ft1v-w181

var.name: Q113aMst
var.labl: Person who earns the most money
var.imputation: Person who earns the most money
var.security: Person who earns the most money
var.embargo: Person who earns the most money
var.respUnit: Person who earns the most money

catgry.labl: Married or living together as husband and wife
Physical, mathematical, computer and life sciences
More than 1 week - less than 2 weeks
Physical, mathematical, computer and life sciences
Adult basic education and training/literacy classes
Other than any of the above

var.name: Q110DLrn
var.labl: Studying through attending classes or distance learning
var.imputation: Studying through attending classes or distance learning
var.security: Studying through attending classes or distance learning
var.embargo: Studying through attending classes or distance learning
var.respUnit: Studying through attending classes or distance learning

catgry.labl: Physical, mathematical, computer and life sciences

var.name: Q21aOwnB
var.labl: Activity last 7 days: run or own business
var.imputation: Activity last 7 days: run or own business
var.security: Activity last 7 days: run or own business
var.embargo: Activity last 7 days: run or own business
var.respUnit: Activity last 7 days: run or own business

var.name: Q21fCons
var.labl: Activity last 7 days: construction or major repair work
var.imputation: Activity last 7 days: construction or major repair work
var.security: Activity last 7 days: construction or major repair work
var.embargo: Activity last 7 days: construction or major repair work
var.respUnit: Activity last 7 days: construction or major repair work

var.name: Q21hBeg
var.labl: Activity last 7 days: beg for money of food
var.imputation: Activity last 7 days: beg for money of food
var.security: Activity last 7 days: beg for money of food
var.embargo: Activity last 7 days: beg for money of food
var.respUnit: Activity last 7 days: beg for money of food

var.name: Q23Absnt
var.labl: Main reason for absence from activity
var.imputation: Main reason for absence from activity
var.security: Main reason for absence from activity
var.embargo: Main reason for absence from activity
var.respUnit: Main reason for absence from activity

catgry.labl: Caring for family or others (except maternity/paternity leave)
Temporarily laid off/Reduction in economic activity
Later than four weeks from now
Has found a job, but is only starting at a definite date in the future
Lack of skills or qualifications for available jobs
Scholar or student, prefers not to work
Retired and prefers not to seek formal work
Illness, invalid, disabled or unable to work (handicapped)
Too young or too old to work
Seasonal worker, e.g. fruit picker, wool-shearer
Cannot find suitable work (salary, location of work or conditions not satisfactory)
Contract worker, e.g. mine worker resting accordingto contract
Later than four weeks from now

var.name: Q34bBgnB
var.labl: Start any kind of business past 4 weeks
var.imputation: Start any kind of business past 4 weeks
var.security: Start any kind of business past 4 weeks
var.embargo: Start any kind of business past 4 weeks
var.respUnit: Start any kind of business past 4 weeks

catgry.labl: Has been temporarily laid off work
Undergoing training to help find work
No jobs available in the area
Lack of money to pay for transport to look for work
Unable to find work requiring his/her skills
Lost hope of finding any kind of work

var.name: Q312bSup
var.labl: Supported by persons in the household
var.imputation: Supported by persons in the household
var.security: Supported by persons in the household
var.embargo: Supported by persons in the household
var.respUnit: Supported by persons in the household

var.name: Q312cSup
var.labl: Supported by persons not in the household
var.imputation: Supported by persons not in the household
var.security: Supported by persons not in the household
var.embargo: Supported by persons not in the household
var.respUnit: Supported by persons not in the household

var.name: Q312gSup
var.labl: Supported by old age or disability pension
var.imputation: Supported by old age or disability pension
var.security: Supported by old age or disability pension
var.embargo: Supported by old age or disability pension
var.respUnit: Supported by old age or disability pension

catgry.labl: Working for someone else for pay?
Working for one or more private households as a domestic employee, gardener or security guard?
Working on his/her own or on a small family farm/plot or collecting natural products from the forest or sea?
Working on his/her own or with a partner, in any type of business (including commercial farms)?
Helping without pay in a family business?
Both the employer and the person him/herself
Tools and/or equipment are rented/hired or owned by an outside person or organisation
The establishment/enterprise/individual for which he/she works

var.name: Q411Pens
var.labl: Contribution to pension or retirement fund
var.imputation: Contribution to pension or retirement fund
var.security: Contribution to pension or retirement fund
var.embargo: Contribution to pension or retirement fund
var.respUnit: Contribution to pension or retirement fund

catgry.labl: A non-profit organisation (NGO, CBO, charity, embassy)
No - because his/her income is above UIF limit
Yes, for him/herself and his/her dependants
Inside a formal business premises such as factory or office
At a service outlet such as a shop, school, post office, etc
On a footpath, street, street corner, open space or field
In the informal sector (including domestic work)

var.name: Q420aHrs
var.labl: Hours worked in main job/activity in past 7 days
var.imputation: Hours worked in main job/activity in past 7 days
var.security: Hours worked in main job/activity in past 7 days
var.embargo: Hours worked in main job/activity in past 7 days
var.respUnit: Hours worked in main job/activity in past 7 days

var.name: Q420bHrs
var.labl: Hours worked in all other work activities in past 7 days
var.imputation: Hours worked in all other work activities in past 7 days
var.security: Hours worked in all other work activities in past 7 days
var.embargo: Hours worked in all other work activities in past 7 days
var.respUnit: Hours worked in all other work activities in past 7 days

var.name: Q420cHrs
var.labl: Hours worked in total in past 7 days
var.imputation: Hours worked in total in past 7 days
var.security: Hours worked in total in past 7 days
var.embargo: Hours worked in total in past 7 days
var.respUnit: Hours worked in total in past 7 days

var.name: Q421aUsu
var.labl: Hours usually worked in main job/activity
var.imputation: Hours usually worked in main job/activity
var.security: Hours usually worked in main job/activity
var.embargo: Hours usually worked in main job/activity
var.respUnit: Hours usually worked in main job/activity

var.name: Q421bUsu
var.labl: Hours usually worked in all other activities
var.imputation: Hours usually worked in all other activities
var.security: Hours usually worked in all other activities
var.embargo: Hours usually worked in all other activities
var.respUnit: Hours usually worked in all other activities

catgry.labl: He/she can decide fully for him/herself
He/she can decide, but within a limited range (e.g. flexitime)
Number of hours are fixed by the employer

var.name: Q425HntX
var.labl: Action to look for extra work
var.imputation: Action to look for extra work
var.security: Action to look for extra work
var.embargo: Action to look for extra work
var.respUnit: Action to look for extra work

catgry.labl: The same work with more hours
As a main source of food for the household
As the main source of income/earning a living
As an extra source of income
As a leisure activity or hobby, e.g. gardening
Financial intermediation, insurance, real estate and business services
Service workers and shop and market sales workers
Plant and machine operators and assemblers
Financial intermediation, insurance, real estate and business services
Service workers and shop and market sales workers
Plant and machine operators and assemblers
